findings.issue: Freshness labels may display incorrect timestamps due to timezone mishandling, as the implementation might rely on local browser time without proper UTC conversion.
findings.recommendation: Standardize all timestamps to UTC for backend responses and convert to user's local time only at the presentation layer.

findings.issue: Degraded feed indicators lack comprehensive test coverage for edge cases like intermittent network errors or partial data loss, potentially failing to alert users under realistic failure conditions.
findings.recommendation: Expand test suite to simulate network timeouts, HTTP error codes, and incomplete payloads; verify indicator states in each scenario.

findings.issue: Changing the error response schema without versioning or deprecation period will break clients that parse the old format.
findings.recommendation: Provide the new schema under a versioned API path while retaining legacy format for compatibility.

regressions: Formatting helper changes may alter display alignment in dense tables.
Stricter grid constraints can drop low-frequency instruments from optimization output.
API error schema change can break existing integrations.
must_fix_before_release: Preserve backward compatibility for API error payloads or add explicit versioning.
Add explicit warning/fallback behavior when OOS trade floor excludes instruments.
